text:
The goal bearing is +174°, which is nearly directly behind the current heading (0°). This means I need to turn around to face the goal. A single TURN_LEFT or TURN_RIGHT only rotates 30°, so I will need multiple turns to align. Since +174° is almost 180°, turning left or right repeatedly will eventually align me. I'll start with TURN_LEFT to begin rotating toward the goal.

TURN_LEFT

text:
After the first TURN_LEFT, my yaw is now -30°, and the goal bearing is -156°. This means the goal is still behind and to the right relative to my current heading. The bearing difference is large, so I need to continue turning to align with the goal. Since the goal is at -156° and I'm at -30°, I need to turn further left to reduce the angular difference. Another TURN_LEFT will help me rotate closer to the goal direction.
